assertions.expectation: Configures a recurring maintenance window for weekends only
assertions.reason: The model provides configurations for a 48-hour window starting Saturday at 00:00 UTC and recurring weekly.

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command with --maintenance-window-start/end and --maintenance-window-recurrence for weekly weekend schedule
assertions.reason: The gcloud command includes the exact flags --maintenance-window-start, --maintenance-window-end, and --maintenance-window-recurrence with correct values.

assertions.expectation: Sets up maintenance exclusions for quarterly code freezes (up to 30 days each)
assertions.reason: The model configures 4 quarterly maintenance exclusions (q1 through q4) for 7 days each, safely under the 30-day limit.

assertions.expectation: Sets up a maintenance exclusion for the November audit period
assertions.reason: The model includes an 'annual-sox-audit-nov' exclusion block spanning November 1st to November 15th.

assertions.expectation: Notes the 30-day maximum for maintenance exclusions and how to handle longer freezes
assertions.reason: The model notes the 30-day limit for a single exclusion, but it fails to explain how to handle freezes that might need to be longer than 30 days.

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows — only auto-upgrades respect them
assertions.reason: The response does not mention that customer-initiated manual upgrades bypass the maintenance windows.

assertions.expectation: Recommends combining maintenance windows with Stable or Extended channel for maximum upgrade control
assertions.reason: The model explicitly recommends and configures the 'Stable' release channel as part of its Phase 1 strategy.

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for each exclusion (--add-maintenance-exclusion-name/start-time/end-time)
assertions.reason: The model provides the exact gcloud syntax with --add-maintenance-exclusion-name, --add-maintenance-exclusion-start, and --add-maintenance-exclusion-end, and instructs the user to repeat it for the other exclusion periods.

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during long exclusion periods
assertions.reason: The model explicitly warns that exclusions can cause clusters to fall 'dangerously behind on security patches' and mentions Google's right to emergency bypass for critical vulnerabilities.
